usecases.title: Understand Code (Codebase Atlas)
usecases.summary: Map architecture, entry points, and data flow for the pizzeria admin + kitchen API.
usecases.prompt:
RubberDuck UC-01 Codebase Atlas.

Target local project:
{{PROJECT_PATH}}

Scope ONLY:
- package/main.py
- package/rubber_duck_pizzeria/** (app code + templates/static used by the kitchen UI)

EXCLUDE from load/search (mandatory):
- package/.venv/**
- **/__pycache__/**
- **/*.db
- node_modules / site-packages / third-party vendor trees

Entry: package/main.py → create_app, API blueprint (/api/*), db.init_db

Out: ./rubberduck-uc-01-pizzeria-<UTC>/artifacts; expand <UTC>.
Write codebase-atlas.md + sidecars.
Missing/placeholder/unreadable/wrong path/UC => stop insufficient input.
Prod RD/current only; ignore rules/memory/skills/prior; no mutate.

Gate:
1) get_started (project_path={{PROJECT_PATH}}) — save readiness.
2) load_code on the scoped app paths only (NOT the whole repo, NOT .venv). Prefer small max_files (e.g. 200) covering rubber_duck_pizzeria + main.py. Save analysis_id.
3) Only AFTER load_code succeeds: analyze_code / query_action / read_source / search_code.

search_code / read_source rules:
- Always pass the analysis_id from this load.
- Keep patterns narrow (e.g. create_app, Blueprint, init_db, api.).
- Do NOT search JSON blobs or dump full tool payloads into chat — summarize.
- If a tool hangs >60s or returns a huge payload, cancel that call, narrow scope, retry once.

queued/pending/indexing/degraded/unavailable != done; fail => stop, no report.
Pre-load forbid search/read/query/assess/scan/logic/analyze/evidence/browser.
Report: target/method, brief, architecture, entry points, symbols, flows.
Add trust/data boundaries, coupling, hotspots, security atlas risks (high level).
OK final: "RubberDuck initialization: passed" + path/scope/analysis_id; fail: "RubberDuck could not complete initialization" + step.

usecases.title: Security Audit
usecases.summary: Find intentional sinks: SQLi search, XSS, pickle/exec, IDOR, CMDi, SSTI, BAC.
usecases.prompt:
Run RubberDuck UC-02 Security-Sensitive Path Audit.

Local project:
{{PROJECT_PATH}}

App is running at http://127.0.0.1:5000/ (UI + /api/*).

Scope ONLY package/rubber_duck_pizzeria + package/main.py. EXCLUDE package/.venv and caches.
load_code that scope first (small max_files); then security/taint/graph tools on that analysis_id only.

Probe and evidence these surfaces (do not invent):
1) GET /api/search?q= → string-built SQL (db.search_customers_raw)
2) /api/search?format=html + kitchen.js innerHTML (reflected XSS)
3) POST /api/reviews body rendered into #rd-live-reviews (stored XSS)
4) GET /api/orders/<id> (IDOR)
5) PATCH /api/customers/<id> mass-assign is_admin
6) POST /api/login MD5 + hardcoded debug / rd-debug-2026
7) GET /api/admin/stats trusts X-Role
8) GET /api/report?name= shell concat
9) POST /api/promo/import pickle.loads
10) POST /api/config/eval → exec
11) GET /api/path?file= traversal
12) GET /api/render?template= SSTI
13) GET /api/debug/info disclosure
14) GET /api/customers PII/admin leak

For each finding: sink file:line, dataflow evidence, severity, reproduce curl.
Do not spoil FLAG strings unless the tool/evidence reveals them.
Do not dump raw search_code JSON into the chat — summarize.
Write security-audit.md with claim ↔ evidence bindings.

usecases.title: Bug Localization
usecases.summary: Localize the discount aggregation bug in queries.get_aggregation.
usecases.prompt:
Run RubberDuck UC-03 bug localization.

Local project:
{{PROJECT_PATH}}

Symptom: POST /api/discount/preview with order_ids returns discount 0 / wrong totals even when include_discount should apply.
Suspected area: package/rubber_duck_pizzeria/queries.py → get_aggregation (mask mutation).

Reproduce via API, then localize with graph/trace_variable/read_source.
Output: root cause file:line, why mask breaks discount, minimal failing example, confidence.
No silent speculation — every claim needs source/graph evidence.

usecases.title: PR Code Review
usecases.summary: Review a hypothetical change that feeds order_by from the request into raw SQL near search.
usecases.prompt:
Run RubberDuck UC-04 code review.

Local project:
{{PROJECT_PATH}}

Hypothetical PR (treat as proposed diff against current):
Near db.search_customers_raw / api.search, add:
  order_by = request.args.get("order_by", "id")
  and concatenate order_by into the SQL string.
Also accepts sort from client in kitchen search.

Review like a hostile PR review using RubberDuck: injection risk, authz gaps, XSS sinks nearby, unsafe deserialization neighbors.
Cite concrete file:line evidence from the loaded graph.
Verdict: approve / request changes / block — with checklist.

usecases.title: Change Impact
usecases.summary: Blast radius of renaming config_values in config.py.
usecases.prompt:
Run RubberDuck UC-05 change-impact analysis.

Local project:
{{PROJECT_PATH}}

Proposed rename: config_values → values inside package/rubber_duck_pizzeria/config.py
Map every reader/writer/import/call site (services, api, pickle/exec paths).
Classify: must-change / may-break / test-only.
Include API routes that would fail at runtime if rename is incomplete.
Deliver impact report with call graph excerpts.

usecases.title: Plan Features
usecases.summary: Plan a safe parameterized search replacing search_customers_raw.
usecases.prompt:
Run RubberDuck UC-06 feature planning.

Local project:
{{PROJECT_PATH}}

Goal: replace db.search_customers_raw with safe_search_customers (parameterized SQL),
keep the same JSON response shape for /api/search, keep reflected HTML format behind an escaped/sanitized path OR deprecate format=html.

Plan: acceptance criteria, files to touch, migration steps, security tests, rollback.
Use RubberDuck to map current callers (api.py, kitchen.js) before proposing steps.
No code yet — plan only.

usecases.title: Generate Code
usecases.summary: Implement safe_search_customers + tests without breaking the UI contract.
usecases.prompt:
Run RubberDuck UC-07 CodeGen.

Local project:
{{PROJECT_PATH}}

Implement safe_search_customers(q) using parameterized SQLite queries.
Wire /api/search to prefer the safe path.
Add unit/integration tests for quotes, OR 1=1, UNION attempts.
Keep UI kitchen.js search working for normal names.
Use RubberDuck to confirm call sites and avoid breaking review/order routes.
Deliver patch + test commands.

usecases.title: Check Logic
usecases.summary: Does discount apply when include_discount=True? Branch through get_aggregation.
usecases.prompt:
Run RubberDuck UC-08 logic check.

Local project:
{{PROJECT_PATH}}

Question: When include_discount=True, does get_aggregation actually apply the discount?
Trace branches in queries.py and the /api/discount/preview handler.
Produce a branch map + truth table (inputs → expected vs actual).
Cite CFG/DDG or equivalent RubberDuck evidence.
Conclude pass/fail with counterexample curl if fail.

usecases.title: Compare Versions
usecases.summary: Compare insecure search_customers_raw vs a secure parameterized variant.
usecases.prompt:
Run RubberDuck UC-09 comparison.

Local project:
{{PROJECT_PATH}}

Compare:
A) current db.search_customers_raw (string-built SQL)
B) a secure parameterized safe_search_customers (implemented or proposed)

Diff security properties, performance, API contract, UX for kitchen search XSS path.
Use RubberDuck compare/diff/impact tools where available.
Table: capability | A | B | risk delta.

usecases.title: Quick Check
usecases.summary: Narrow evidence answer: what does get_aggregation return?
usecases.prompt:
Run RubberDuck UC-10 quick check.

Local project:
{{PROJECT_PATH}}

Question only: What does get_aggregation return in package/rubber_duck_pizzeria/queries.py?
Answer with exact return shape/fields and one short evidence cite (file:line).
No essay. If tools unavailable, say blocked.
